target.title: fix(whatsapp): align message hook config
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #86390 by accepting documented WhatsApp channel/account `pluginHooks.messageReceived` config in the strict channel schema.
- Makes the WhatsApp runtime also honor the manifest-backed plugin config path `plugins.entries.whatsapp.config.pluginHooks.messageReceived`.
- Regenerates bundled channel config metadata, documents precedence, and adds regression coverage for schema validation and hook emission.

## Tests

- `tmpdir=$(mktemp -d); config="$tmpdir/config.json5"; printf '%s\n' '{' '  channels: {' '    whatsapp: {' '      pluginHooks: { messageReceived: true },' '      accounts: {' '        work: { pluginHooks: { messageReceived: true } }' '      }' '    }' '  }' '}' > "$config"; OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$config" pnpm openclaw config validate --json; status=$?; rm -rf "$tmpdir"; exit $status`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts src/config/config.allowlist-requires-allowfrom.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/config-schema.test.ts`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-whatsapp.ts src/config/config.allowlist-requires-allowfrom.test.ts src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp inbound `message_received` hook opt-in config now has a schema-valid documented channel/account path, and the runtime also honors the plugin manifest config path.
Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout on commit c63c06e66b, Node via repo toolchain, running the real OpenClaw CLI against an isolated temporary config file.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `tmpdir=$(mktemp -d); config="$tmpdir/config.json5"; printf '%s\n' '{' '  channels: {' '    whatsapp: {' '      pluginHooks: { messageReceived: true },' '      accounts: {' '        work: { pluginHooks: { messageReceived: true } }' '      }' '    }' '  }' '}' > "$config"; OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$config" pnpm openclaw config validate --json; status=$?; rm -rf "$tmpdir"; exit $status`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts src/config/config.allowlist-requires-allowfrom.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/config-schema.test.ts`.
Evidence after fix: The real CLI command printed `{"valid":true,"path":"/var/folders/hy/bphml7v52zxdq_8_273nn9ph0000gn/T/tmp.ihBxfmmjh2/config.json5"}` and exited 0 after building/syncing the local source checkout; targeted Vitest exited 0.
Observed result after fix: `openclaw config validate --json` accepts documented `channels.whatsapp.pluginHooks.messageReceived` and account-scoped `channels.whatsapp.accounts.<id>.pluginHooks.messageReceived`, while runtime hook emission coverage verifies the plugin-entry config path and account-level precedence.
What was not tested: No live WhatsApp/Baileys connection was used; the runtime proof uses OpenClaw's actual CLI validator with an isolated config file plus the WhatsApp processMessage runtime harness with mocked transport/hook runner.
target.author: leno23
